articles.title: Crude oil backs off a bit after reaching the 61.8% retracement level target
articles.expand: The price of crude oil pushed to its highest level since March 9, extending to an intraday high of $103.86. In doing so, the price moved above the 61.8% retracement of the sharp move down from the March 9 high to the March 10 low, which comes in at $103.16—tilting the bias more firmly in favor of buyers. Looking back to last week, the price had alr...

articles.title: Major European Markets Close On Firm Note Despite War Concerns
articles.expand: (RTTNews) - Despite weak Eurozone economic sentiment data and the ongoing war in the Middle East, European stocks gained some significant ground in positive territory on Monday. The upmove was supported by some bargain hunting at several counters following recent declines.

articles.title: Dollar Supported by Concerns of a Protracted Iran War
articles.expand: The dollar index (DXY00 ) climbed to a 2-week high today and is up by +0.35%. The dollar has safe-haven support on concerns of a protracted Iran war after President Trump told the Financial Times on Sunday that he wants to "take the oil in Iran" and could seize the...

articles.expand: The British Pound collapses on Monday as Middle East escalations push the Greenback higher, while Oil prices extend their gains for the fourth consecutive trading day. At the time of writing, the GBP/USD trades at 1.3184, down by more than 0.50%. Read More...

articles.title: GBP/USD dives to four-month lows as Middle East tensions lift the US Dollar
articles.expand: The British Pound (GBP) collapses on Monday as Middle East escalations push the US Dollar (USD) higher, while Oil prices extend their gains for the fourth consecutive trading day. At the time of writing, the GBP/USD trades at 1.3184, down by more than 0.50%, hitting a four-month low.

articles.expand: S P 500, Dow Jones, Nasdaq Analysis and Trading Levels: US benchmarks rally as Fed Chair Jerome Powell offers an optimistic economic outlook during his Harvard speech. While WTI prices and the US Dollar rise, President Trump eases anxiety over an extended Middle East conflict. Explore an intraday technical analysis of the markets.

articles.title: Gold holds firm as yields ease, US Dollar strength caps gains
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) struggles to gain traction on Monday, trimming part of its earlier gains as escalating tensions in the Middle East and shifting interest rate expectations keep markets volatile.

articles.expand: President Trump spoke with the New York Post in some comments that are hard to interpret. He seemed to indicate that the US was negotiating with Iran's parliamentary speaker, someone who was reported dead on the weekend but has continued to tweet frequently, including about market moves. articles.expand: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell struck a tone of “strategic ambiguity” in remarks at a Harvard event, signaling that policymakers are not yet ready to respond to the current oil-driven supply shock, even as risks to both sides of the mandate are building. As the Iran war extends, Powell acknowledged the Fed could eventually face […] The p...

articles.title: Euro area: Inflation jump and hawkish ECB stance – Societe Generale
articles.expand: Societe Generale economists expect Euro area headline inflation to rise sharply in March, driven by higher energy costs, while core inflation eases slightly.

articles.title: Powell speech: Policy in good place to wait and see
articles.expand: Federal Reserve (Fed) Chairman Jerome Powell said on Monday that there is tension between the Fed's two objectives, during a moderated discussion at the Harvard University Principles of Economics Class in Cambridge, per Reuters.

articles.title: Oil: Escalation risks from Iran conflict – NBC
articles.expand: National Bank of Canada’s (NBC) Angelo Katsoras outlines how an Iran conflict could severely disrupt Oil and gas markets if key energy infrastructure and the Strait of Hormuz are targeted.

articles.expand: Societe Generale economists note the United Kingdom (UK) markets still price around 70 bps of Bank of England (BoE) easing in 2026 despite Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) pushback, while the bank’s team expects no cuts this year.

articles.expand: TD Securities analysts anticipate the Bank of Canada’s (BoC) March Summary of Deliberations will maintain a dovish tone, stressing excess supply and disinflation.

Last week’s turning point came when the pair found support near the 100-bar moving average on the 4-hour chart, bottoming at 1.3669 (just above the MA at 1.3662). From there, the buyers took control. articles.title: Germany: War-driven energy shock risks ECB hike – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Senior Economist Dr. Ralph Solveen notes that Germany’s inflation rate rose from 1.9% to 2.7% in March 2026 as energy prices surged due to the war in Iran, while core inflation stayed at 2.5%.

articles.title: Eurozone: Inflation fears rise on war shock – ING
articles.expand: ING’s Chief Economist Bert Colijn highlights that Eurozone sentiment weakened in March as the Middle East war hit confidence. The economic sentiment indicator dropped from 98.3 in February to 96.6, with businesses and consumers more worried about inflation.

articles.title: Euro area economic sentiment eases in March as Middle East conflict stokes inflation fears
articles.expand: Euro area economic confidence slipped to 96.6 in March, after recording a revised 98.2 reading in February. That's the lowest estimate since September with consumer confidence (-16.3) also falling back on the month to its lowest since the latter months of 2023. It's all coming undone as the US-Iran war stirs up inflation fears in Europe once again....

articles.title: Month-end flows point to dollar buying into the fix - BofA
articles.expand: According to BofA's month-end fixing model, the dollar is the one favoured as we look to wrap up March and Q1 trading in the coming days. This fits with what was pointed out by both Credit Agricole and Barclays earlier here . Of note, BofA says that: "Our estimates suggest the potential for (1) material inflows into USD-denominated assets (c.+1.0σ)...

articles.title: Swiss KOF Barometer Drops Sharply to 96.1, Signals Broad-Based Economic Weakness
articles.expand: Switzerland’s KOF Economic Barometer fell sharply from 103.8 to 96.1 in March, missing expectations of 100.6 and pointing to a notable deterioration in growth momentum. The decline reflects a broad-based slowdown, with both production and demand-side indicators weakening, suggesting that the economy is losing traction more quickly than anticipated....
